description:
## What
Hook into the post create, edit, and delete paths to keep the tantivy index in sync with the posts table.

## Why
Search results are only as good as the index; stale or missing documents produce wrong results and erode user trust.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: read tantivy docs for deleting documents by term (Term::from_field_u64 on the id field, IndexWriter::delete_term); understand how IndexWriter::add_document and delete_term are ordered relative to commit; read tantivy docs for confirming that a commit flushes both adds and deletes atomically.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: in the new-post handler, after successful DB INSERT, call a `search::index_post(&app_state.index_writer, &post)` function that acquires the Mutex, calls add_document with all schema fields populated, releases lock, then spawns a blocking task to call commit; in the edit-post handler, after successful DB UPDATE, acquire lock, call delete_term for the old id, call add_document for the new content, release lock, spawn blocking commit; in the delete-post handler, after DB DELETE, acquire lock, call delete_term for the post id, release lock, spawn blocking commit; add a `search::delete_all_for_persona(persona_id)` function called when a persona is deleted, using a range query on the persona_id FAST field.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write integration tests: (1) create a post containing unique word W, search index for W, assert post id appears; (2) edit post to remove W and add unique word X, search for W assert no result, search for X assert result; (3) delete post, search for X assert no result. Each step commits and opens a new reader to avoid stale cache.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: the test constructs expected post ids from the DB row returned by INSERT — the search result is verified against the DB-returned id, not re-derived from the search code itself.
- Test approach: three sequential subtests as above; each subtest opens a fresh IndexReader via index.reader() after commit to ensure committed state is queried.

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- Index write failures must be logged at ERROR but must not fail the HTTP request — the DB write is the source of truth; index is a derived view
- Never hold Mutex<IndexWriter> across an await point
- Commit is spawned in spawn_blocking; errors from commit are logged, not propagated to the request handler
- delete_all_for_persona must run inside a spawn_blocking call, not inline in an async handler
- Visibility field must be set correctly on index — used by search to filter results
title: Index posts on create, edit, and delete
